programme.title: Mad Max
programme.desc: Taking place in a dystopian Australia in the near future, Mad Max tells the story of a highway patrolman cruising the squalid back roads that have become the breeding ground of criminals foraging for gasoline and scraps. After some grisly events at the hands of a motorcycle gang, Max sets out across the barren wastelands in search of revenge.

programme.title: Mechanic: Resurrection
programme.desc: Arthur Bishop thought he had put his murderous past behind him when his most formidable foe kidnaps the love of his life. Now he is forced to travel the globe to complete three impossible assassinations, and do what he does best, make them look like accidents.

programme.title: Indiana Jones and the Last Crusade
programme.desc: An art collector appeals to Indiana Jones to embark on a search for the Holy Grail. He learns that another archaeologist has disappeared while searching for the precious goblet, and the missing man is his own father, Dr. Henry Jones. The artifact is much harder to find than they expected, and its powers are too much for those impure of heart.

programme.title: Baby Driver
programme.desc: Coerced into working for a crime boss, a young getaway driver must face the music when a doomed heist threatens his life, love, and freedom.

programme.title: Constantine
programme.desc: John Constantine has literally been to Hell and back. When he teams up with a policewoman to solve the mysterious suicide of her twin sister, their investigation takes them through the world of demons and angels that exists beneath the landscape of contemporary Los Angeles.

programme.title: The Punisher
programme.desc: After Frank Castle's whole family is gunned down in cold blood, he shall become the vengeful Angel of Death known as the Great Equaliser of Crime: The Punisher. The one-man hurricane of blood and bullets is about to make the thugs who destroyed his life rue the day they made The Punisher come to life.

programme.title: Universal Soldier
programme.desc: An elite team of soldiers has been used against terrorists where they use astounding physical capabilities to overcome them. Victoria, a reporter, follows them and discovers a part of their secret. When one of the team kills her cameraman she tries to escape. Luc, one of the soldiers begins to have flashbacks and turns sides and helps her as the remainder of the team follows to protect their secrets.

programme.title: Mad Max
programme.desc: Taking place in a dystopian Australia in the near future, Mad Max tells the story of a highway patrolman cruising the squalid back roads that have become the breeding ground of criminals foraging for gasoline and scraps. After some grisly events at the hands of a motorcycle gang, Max sets out across the barren wastelands in search of revenge.

programme.title: Sherlock Holmes
programme.desc: Detective Sherlock Holmes and his stalwart partner Watson engage in a battle of wits and brawn with a nemesis whose plot is a threat to all of England.

programme.title: Total Recall
programme.desc: Douglas Quaid is haunted by a recurring dream about a journey to Mars. He hopes to find out more about this dream and buys a holiday at Rekall Inc. where they sell implanted memories. But something goes wrong with the memory implantation and he remembers being a secret agent fighting against the evil Mars administrator Cohaagen. Now the story really begins and it's a rollercoaster ride until the massive end of the movie.

programme.title: The Terminator
programme.desc: In the post-apocalyptic future, reigning tyrannical supercomputers teleport a cyborg assassin known as the "Terminator" back to 1984 to kill Sarah Connor, whose unborn son is destined to lead insurgents against 21st century mechanical hegemony. Meanwhile, the human-resistance movement dispatches a lone warrior to safeguard Sarah. Can he stop the virtually indestructible killing machine?

programme.title: Snatch
programme.desc: Unscrupulous boxing promoters, violent bookmakers, a Russian gangster, incompetent amateur robbers and supposedly Jewish jewelers fight to track down a priceless stolen diamond.

programme.title: Abraham Lincoln: Vampire Hunter
programme.desc: Abraham Lincoln, the 16th President of the United States, discovers vampires are planning to take over the United States. He makes it his mission to eliminate them.

programme.title: John Wick: Chapter 4
programme.desc: John Wick uncovers a path to defeating The High Table. But before he can earn his freedom, Wick must face off against a new enemy with powerful alliances across the globe and forces that turn old friends into foes.

programme.title: House of Frankenstein
programme.desc: After escaping from prison, the mad Dr. Gustav Niemann and his loyal hunchbacked assistant, Daniel, revive not only Count Dracula, but the Wolf Man and Frankenstein's Monster as well, all in order to exact revenge upon Niemann's many enemies.

programme.title: The Old Dark House
programme.desc: An American who sells cars in England receives a mysterious invitation from an old, eccentric millionaire to visit his house in which he lives with his twin brother.

programme.title: House of Wax
programme.desc: A sculptor opens a wax museum to showcase the likenesses of famous historical figures, but quickly runs into trouble when his business partner demands the exhibits become more extreme in order to increase profits.

programme.title: I Bury the Living
programme.desc: Through a series of macabre "coincidences," the newly-elected director of a cemetery (Richard Boone) begins to believe that he can cause the deaths of living owners of burial plots by merely changing the push-pin color from white (living) to black (dead) on a large wall map of the cemetery that notes those plots.

programme.title: Invaders from Mars
programme.desc: A young boy learns that space aliens are taking over the minds of earthlings.

programme.title: Invisible Agent
programme.desc: The Invisible Man's grandson uses his secret formula to spy on Nazi Germany.

programme.title: Island of Terror
programme.desc: A small, isolated island community's terrorised from a horde of creeping, blobbish, tentacled monsters which liquefy and digest the bones from living creatures. The community struggles to fight back, against these horrors.

programme.title: Lady Frankenstein
programme.desc: Baron Frankenstein's daughter and his assistant/her lover continue his experiments in an attempt to rebuild his legacy after he is killed by his psychotic, murderous first monster.

programme.title: Night of the Living Dead
programme.desc: A ragtag group of Pennsylvanians barricade themselves in an old farmhouse to remain safe from a horde of flesh-eating ghouls that are ravaging the Northeast of the United States.

programme.title: Renfield
programme.desc: Renfield, Dracula's tortured henchman, is forced to capture prey for his master and do his every bidding. But now, after centuries of servitude, Renfield is ready to see if there is a life outside his boss's shadow.

programme.title: Son of Frankenstein
programme.desc: Returning to the ancestral castle long after the death of the monster, the son of Dr. Frankenstein meets a mad shepherd who is hiding the comatose creature. To clear the family name, he revives the creature and tries to rehabilitate him.

programme.title: Soylent Green
programme.desc: A nightmarish futuristic fantasy about the controlling power of big corporations and an innocent cop who stumbles on the truth.

programme.title: Terror Train
programme.desc: A college fraternity prank goes wrong and a student ends up in the mental asylum. Three years later, it's graduation time, and the members of the fraternity decide to have a costume party aboard a train trip to celebrate their graduation. Unknowingly to them, a killer has slipped aboard, killing them off one by one, disguised in the costumes of the victims.

programme.title: The Dead Don't Die
programme.desc: The focus is on the sleepy small town of Centerville where the dead become undead.

programme.title: The Ghost of Frankenstein
programme.desc: Ygor resurrects Frankenstein's monster and brings him to the original doctor's son Ludwig for help. Ludwig, obsessed with the idea of restoring the monster to full power, is unaware that his various associates all have different ideas about whose brain should be transplanted into the monster's skull.

programme.title: The Invisible Man
programme.desc: A scientist finds a way of becoming invisible, but in doing so, he becomes murderously insane.

programme.title: The Invisible Man Returns
programme.desc: The owner of a coal mining operation, falsely imprisoned for fratricide, takes a drug to make him invisible, despite its side effect: gradual madness.

programme.title: The Invisible Man's Revenge
programme.desc: An eager scientist tests his new formula for invisibility on an escaped fugitive. When the formula works the criminal runs off to terrorize a family he believes cheated him out of a fortune years earlier.

programme.title: Tom Petty and the Heartbreakers - Rockpalast (1977)
Jethro Tull - Jack In The Green
ACDC - Live in Cleveland Full Concert (2003)
Ramones - Live At The Rainbow - December 31, 1977
Eric Clapton - Old Grey Whistle test (1977)
The Who-Tommy Live Universal Amphitheatre 1989 Second Set
WWE St Valentine's Day Massacre In Your House 1999
Columbo
programme.desc: One member of a mystery-writing-team decides to kill his more talented partner when the better writer decides to go solo.

programme.title: Maude
programme.desc: Maude and Walter are scheduled to play cards one evening with some friends but Maude is anything but in a card playing mood. Especially since, she just came back from a doctor's appointment with some shocking news. At 47 years old, Maude has become pregnant. Now the hard part, breaking the news to Walter.

programme.title: Maude
programme.desc: After Carol suggests an abortion, Maude begins heavy debating over the pros and cons of having a baby at her age. Walter's ""what-ever-you-want"" attitude has Maude having a problem deciding wheather or not to keep the baby. Ultimately, Maude takes into consideration everyone's opinion and makes a final decision, she decides not to have the baby.

programme.title: The Golden Girls
programme.desc: Dorothy dumps a depressed Stan on Blanche but then becomes incredibly jealous when Blanche and Stan hit it off. Meanwhile, Sophia and Rose begin selling sandwiches on the Boardwalk but they inadvertently make an enemy of another vendor, "Johnny No Thumbs".

programme.title: The Golden Girls
programme.desc: Rose's petition to save a very old tree attracts the ire of Freida Claxton, a misanthropic old woman, on whose property the tree stands. At a public hearing, Rose also gets angry at Mrs. Claxton and tells her to "drop dead," which she does.

programme.title: The Golden Girls
programme.desc: Dorothy's old friend, Jean comes to Miami for a visit and Dorothy decides not to tell Blanche and Rose that Jean is a lesbian. Then, after spending time with Rose, Jean begins to fall for her. Meanwhile, a new VCR, prompts Sophia to rent ""dirty movies.""

programme.title: The Golden Palace
programme.desc: Blanche and Roland clash over who is the boss of the hotel, meanwhile Rose unknowingly opens up the hotel to a serial killer.

programme.title: Sanford and Son
programme.desc: One evening, Lamont and his friend Rollo go out. When they return they interrupt a robber trying to flee from the scene. Later they discover the crook dropped his gun and in an attempt to get some quick cash, decide to pawn it. However, once at the pawn shop, the broker mistakes Fred wanting money for the gun for a stickup.

programme.title: All in the Family
programme.desc: Archie Baptizes Baby Joey without Mike and Gloria's Permission.

programme.title: All in the Family
programme.desc: Mike finds out that Archie baptized Joey without his consent, prompting another argument between the agnostic and the so-called Christian. The argument keeps heating up--until the Bunkers' furnace breaks down.

programme.title: All in the Family
programme.desc: Edith abandons her stick-in-the-mud husband to liven things up on her own during an evening out at Kelly's Bar.

programme.title: Supermarket Sweep
programme.sub-title: Grace & Lon vs. Sharon & George vs. Christine & Diana

programme.title: Supermarket Sweep
programme.sub-title: Janis & Richard vs. Doug & Joann vs. Jeannie & Kris

programme.title: The Three Stooges
programme.desc: The stooges go out west for Shemp's health and get mixed up with some bad guys. The villains have locked up the Arizona Kid and their leader plans to marry his girl, Nell. The boys help the Arizona Kid escape and he rides to fetch the Cavalry. Somehow, the stooges manage to defeat the bad guys before the Cavalry arrives.

programme.title: The Three Stooges
programme.desc: The stooges are janitors working in a movie studio. After wrecking the bosses office, they get jobs as actors in an African movie. Curly plays a gorilla and Moe and Larry are primitive natives. On location in Africa, the stooges have a confrontation with a witch doctor from whom Curly buys some "love candy" with hopes of attracting the films leading lady. When a female gorilla disrupts the movie set, Curly eats some of the candy and chases after her.

programme.title: The Three Stooges
programme.desc: The stooges are delivering some Arabian antiques, which include a magic lamp complete with genie. Three Arabian bad guys are after the magic lamp, but the stooges defeat them once they get the "genius", (as Shemp calls the genie) on their side.

programme.title: The Three Stooges
programme.desc: An eccentric scientist tricks the stooges into joining himself and his daughter on an expedition to Venus. On Venus, the boys go exploring and encounter some cannibalistic amazons who plan to devour them. The stooges escape and take off in the spaceship which goes wildly out of control. As the ship is about to crash, the scene changes to the annual meeting of the Liars Club, where the stooges win the prize as the biggest liars in the world.

programme.title: The Three Stooges
programme.desc: The stooges are working as paperhangers in the home of Professor Panzer, a mad scientist looking for a brain to use in his experiments. The professor wants to put a human brain into a gorilla but has trouble finding a brain small enough, which leads him to select Curly (for obvious reasons) as the perfect donor. The stooges manage to foil the madman with the help of the Gorilla who befriends Curly.

programme.title: The Three Stooges
programme.desc: Shemp dies but cannot get into heaven until he reforms Moe and Larry. He returns to earth as an invisible spirit and sets out to prevent the other two stooges from selling a phony invention (a fountain pen that writes under whip cream) to a rich couple. Shemp sabotages Moe and Larry's sales pitch, but it looks he's headed for the fires below anyway.

programme.title: The Three Stooges
programme.desc: The stooges become detectives and go to the aid of girl in the clutches of a mad scientist. The boys arrive at a spooky mansion where the madman is building a mechanical man that needs a human head. After declining the opportunity to supply a stooge-head for the experiment, they find the girl and escape, only to wind up in a car driven by the headless robot.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: Becky volunteers to take over cooking duties for the Conners' Thanksgiving; Dan and Mark are still at each other's throats; Fred is shocked at the dysfunction of the family he might marry into; and Nana Mary reveals a long-kept secret about Bev's marriage.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: Leon convinces Jackie and Nancy to open the restaurant for breakfast, causing Roseanne to fear he is planning to force her out. Roseanne's car is stolen -- by D.J., and her violent reaction frightens her that she may repeat the pattern of her own abuse as a child, while Dan blames her anger on work stress.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: Roseanne and Dan snub the neighborhood association by putting up gaudy Xmas decorations, and become furious with Becky when she gives the check they have given her for college to Mark and takes a job as a waitress in a tacky bar.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: Roseanne joins a women's club in hopes of establishing useful contacts, leaving D.J. high and dry with a weird classmate whose mother also belongs. Meanwhile, Mark drops a bombshell on Becky when Dan discovers that he has dropped out of school.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: Dan and Roseanne try to talk Mark into coming back to Becky; meanwhile, Roseanne finds out that David is living with Darlene and heads for Chicago to confront them.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: Guilt and Roseanne's slavedriving push David to confess to Dan that he was living with Darlene in Chicago, and Dan throws him out; Jackie refuses to take money for the baby from Fred; Roseanne and Becky try to pressure Dan to hire Mark.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: Jackie's house buzzes with activity as Roseanne tries to hide David from Dan.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: Roseanne becomes involved in a family feud as Fred sues Jackie for custody of their unborn child; Mark and D.J. tease David for being unmanly.

programme.title: Are You Being Served?
programme.desc: In order to qualify for a new pension plan, the staff have to undergo a medical examination. The men's department is embarrassed about having to undress in front of each other, and decide to try dancing to get in shape.

programme.title: Are You Being Served?
programme.desc: By permission of Mr. Grace, Mrs. Slocombe moves into a display apartment in Grace Brothers, because her new home is occupied by squatters. Due to a transport strike, Mr. Humphries seeks to join her in her new accommodations.

programme.title: Toon in With Me
programme.desc:
In another "Fantastic Friday", Bill and Toony take requests and answer mail from viewers.
Featured cartoons: Hillbilly Hare (1950) Blackboard Jumble (1957) She Was an Acrobat's Daughter (1937) Shakespearian Spinach (1940) The Solid Tin Coyote (1966)

programme.title: Columbo
programme.desc: Columbo matches his skills against Brimmer, a former cop turned private investigator with a quick temper who tries to blackmail a client's wife. When she refuses, he accidentally kills her and it's up to Columbo to nail him.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Max and Caroline are unable to pay the rent on their shop. A person offers to take their lease but Caroline thinks they should try to find the money. So she decides to ask her Aunt who's a successful businesswoman. But she refuses to help them. They try to ask her again but she's on medication and she reveals that she hates Caroline which is why she doesn't want to help her.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Max and Caroline take office temp jobs. While Caroline tries to climb the corporate ladder, Max deals with a boss who makes inappropriate advances.

programme.title: WWF Prime Time Wrestling
programme.desc: Bobby "The Brain" Heenan joins Gorilla Monsoon to host another exciting and action-packed edition of Prime Time Wrestling, featuring King Haku taking on Hillbilly Jim in the main event. Plus, The Brain Busters in action, Greg "The Hammer" Valentine takes on "Rugged" Ronnie Garvin, and much more.

programme.title: Bob's Burgers
programme.desc: When Bob inherits a storage unit around Christmas, visions of riches dance in the heads of the family, but what they find inside is a squatter who claims he once worked as a mannequin.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Ray and Debra have differing points of view on how best to handle the situation, when they learn that Ally has been bullying another child on the school bus. Ray thinks Ally's pushiness comes from Debra, and she thinks that if she doesn't instil independence in Ally, she'll end up being a wuss, just like her Raymond.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Everyone gets on Ray's case because he's doesn't go to Sunday Mass. When Ray starts feeling guilty and sees the good example his father Frank is setting by attending church, he commits to becoming a regular parishioner. But when he shows up at the church, he soon figures out the real reason his father likes attending Mass.

programme.title: The King of Queens
programme.desc: When Doug learns that Carrie is buying him clothing from the "Big and Tall Shop", he is now determined to lose weight. While he is on his journey to lose weight, he tells Carrie that she needs to make some changes to her lifestyle as well. Meanwhile, Arthur is trying to keep the family safe by getting a safety dummy.

programme.title: The King of Queens
programme.desc: When Carrie spends a night out with some of her colleagues, she finds out that they are more successful then she is. Carrie never went to college. Now, she is trying very hard to get back to school and get a good education to become more successful. But will she be able to balance work, school, and family at the same time?

programme.title: Seinfeld
programme.desc: Jerry has a hard time telling the pool guy at his new health club that there isn't room for a 5th member of the group. George's worlds collide when Elaine starts hanging out with Susan. Kramer discovers that his phone number is one digit off from that of a popular movie-finding service.

programme.title: Seinfeld
programme.desc: Elaine holds a monopoly on the contraceptive devices that Susan prefers; Kramer refuses to wear a ribbon on his AIDS walk.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: Audrey cries to Dan about his father owing her money, so he confronts Ed, hoping he will do the right thing. Jackie and Fred go out on a "get-to-know-you-better" date to try to build an honest, solid relationship, but Jackie's admission that she's had many more sexual partners than he has causes a big step backward in their fragile relationship. D.J. goes through a rebellious stage.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: Roseanne helps Dan understand his dad's lying about his mom's mental state.

programme.title: The Office (US)
programme.desc: While Michael and Dwight are out of the office for the day trying to close a deal on a new condo for Michael, Jim and Pam arrange for the staff to participate in an "Office Olympics," where the workers play strange, invented games (among them, Dunderball, Hate Ball...and Pam Pong) that they usually play while the boss is out. At the end of the day, the festivities cheer up everyone - even the oblivious Michael.

programme.title: The Office (US)
programme.desc: The Office is forced into the parking lot for the day when a fire starts in the kitchen. To ease everyone's boredom, Jim suggests a game of "Desert Island" and "Who Would You Do?", where certain staff members reveal a little too much information about themselves. Throughout the day, Michael talks business with Ryan, leaving Dwight feeling left out.

programme.title: The Office (US)
programme.desc: Corporate demands a Halloween layoff, leaving Michael in a two-headed costume dodging decisions while Dwight and Jim's résumé battle, and Creed's survival instincts, turn the spooky day into real dread.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: A snowstorm traps the students of Springfield Elementary inside, with them overthrowing Principal Skinner when he uses his Army skills to control them. Meanwhile, Homer tries to rescue the children–using Flanders' car.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: While working as a human guinea pig (to pay off the family's lost savings after making a bad investment), Homer discovers the root cause of his subnormal intelligence: a crayon that was lodged in his brain ever since he was a boy. He decides to have it removed to increase his IQ, but discovers that being smart does not necessarily equal being happy.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Marge is responsible for getting Jack Crowley,a prisoner with artistic talent, parole and a job at the school,painting a vast and beautiful mural. However Skinner dislikes it and orders a twee replacement. Both the new mural and Skinner's car are consequently torched and Marge is dismayed to think that Jack let her down. Homer experiences a similar lack of success when he tries to market a trash can as a cure for back pains.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Bart and Milhouse are banned from The Android Dungeon after stopping Comic Book Guy from buying a box of priceless Star Wars memorabilia for $5, but are hired as his replacements when Comic Book Guy has a heart attack and is advised to leave his job in order to make friends.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: While making funeral arrangements for Grampa's future, the funeral planner pitches the idea of a mausoleum that uses the same amount of cement as a full-sized tennis court. This prompts Homer to instead build a tennis court in his backyard, initially mistaking the sport for "Foxy Boxing". The tennis court makes Marge and Homer popular in town, but it turns out that this is only because Homer's inferior tennis ability makes them an easy target. Bart on the other hand, shines as a natural.

programme.title: Married... with Children
programme.desc: The way to a man's heart may be through his stomach, but it's not the heart that's sought by Peggy who's tired of being ignored by Al in the bedroom. So she tries a variety of exciting new outfits in an attempt to whet the appetite of a starving Al. But the only thing Al wants is a cooked steak for dinner.

programme.title: Married... with Children
programme.desc: To meet girls, Bud starts his own community college fraternity, "Alpha Gonna Get'Em," with Hindu convenience store clerk Achmed, geek Francis, and retiree Gus as charter (sole) members. But the first toga party, in the Bundy garage, lacks fun and women.

programme.title: Married... with Children
programme.desc: On Election Day, all of the Bundys (minus Seven) hit the voting booths, united to defeat political correctness and a new two cent beer tax. When their causes lose at the polls, the Bundys hit the streets for some good old-fashioned pillaging.

programme.title: Married... with Children
programme.desc: Al decides that another Bundy must get a job and the ax falls on Kelly. This breaks Peggy's heart since no Wanker woman has ever worked before. After taking a job at a greasy spoon diner, Kelly must experience the wonderful world of waitressing prior to her true calling as Kelly Bundy: Philosopher/Waitress.

programme.title: Married... with Children
programme.desc: Al and the family try to sneak into the first class lounge by dressing up like rock stars.

programme.title: Married... with Children
programme.desc: Al buys a cemetery plot so that he will be buried next to a western movie star and a mule. But Peggy feels excluded.

programme.title: Married... with Children
programme.desc: Thanks to a college grant of $25,000, Bud is ready to leave home and start the good life at college, until Al and Peggy discover the money. Being the selfish petty criminals they are, they grant the money for themselves, and go on a wild spending spree in just one day that leaves Bud with zero.

programme.title: Married... with Children
programme.desc: Al remembers how the Bundys celebrated Christmas over the years.

programme.title: Sanford and Son
programme.desc: The diamond thieves continue their plot to have Fred smuggle the stolen diamonds into the United States. Freddy Wells sneaks into Fred's hotel room and plants the diamonds in his suitcase. Later, Lamont and Fred discover the diamonds and hesitate to call the police.

programme.title: Sanford and Son
programme.desc: Fred and Lamont have been busy planting a small garden in their yard when suddenly a professor stumbles in and suggests that they may be sitting on some oil. Later, Fred plants his scarecrow in the garden and actually strikes some oil. This leads to Fred and Lamont to ponder the fact that they may soon be millionaires.

programme.title: Sanford and Son
programme.desc: Fred becomes smitten with a woman, Miss Wallace who comes to the Sanford residence looking for a place to stay at the Sanford Arms. Meanwhile, a thief has been spotted in the neighborhood and to get the reward money, Fred lets the cops set up a stakeout in his home. However, Fred is in for a shock since the thief has been in disguise as Miss Wallace.

programme.title: Get a Life
programme.desc: While on his first jet airplane flight, Chris falls out, and is disappointed when his life flashes before his eyes and it only covers the past eighteen months.

programme.title: Get a Life
programme.desc: Chris talks Larry into taking the day off for the world premiere of the Hell Loop 2000, a 360 degree looped rollercoaster, where they get stuck at the top of the loop. Same story as the TV pilot episode but with a different actress playing Chris' mother.

programme.title: Looney Tunes
programme.sub-title: Bugs Bunny and the Three Bears
programme.desc: The three bears set a trap to catch Goldilocks but their carrot soup attracts Bugs Bunny.

programme.title: Looney Tunes
programme.desc: Porky and Daffy are workers at an aircraft company, and are chronically late. Why? Because they have a great deal of trouble getting to sleep, between the noisy cats, the full moon shining insistently, the sudden rain shower (and leak in the roof).

programme.title: Looney Tunes
programme.desc: Bugs is threatened by a dog that hunts rabbits. While trying to escape, Bugs dresses as a woman, trying to avert the dog's attention off chasing Bugs.

programme.title: Looney Tunes
programme.desc: Porky Pig is hired to babysit.

programme.title: Looney Tunes
programme.desc: Granny takes in a cold and sick Bugs from the terrible weather. Her dog, Sylvester, instantly gets jealous by all the attention she is giving the rabbit. As soon as Granny leaves the room, chaos begins.

programme.title: Looney Tunes
programme.desc: The Masked Marauder (Bugs) is a carrot thief in the West. The only cowboy that can stop him is the dumbest one in the West: Red Hot Ryder.

programme.title: Looney Tunes
programme.desc: Elmer Fudd asks God when he will finally be able to catch Bugs.

programme.title: Looney Tunes
programme.desc: That wascawwy wabbit is chased into a theatre by Elmer Fudd, and ends up having to perform to save himself, as well as convince Elmer to act himself. The vaudeville industry was never this wacky!

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: While doing community service work at the hospital, the boys steal heart patient Billy Bob's golf cart and wreak further havoc.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: McVicker sends Beavis and Butt-head to a group therapy session where they terrorize fellow patients.

programme.title: Mike Judge's Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: Beavis and Butt-Head try acupuncture, and Beavis decides to spend the rest of his life with 50 needles in his face.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.sub-title: Beavis and Butthead - King Turd Collection - https://archive.org/details/beavis-and-butthead-king-turd-collection

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc:
Beavis and Butthead see a milk add on tv which reminds them that cow tipping is cool, so they set out to try it.Videos:
Men Without Hats "Safety Dance"
Fu Schnickens "What's Up Doc"
Violent Femmes "Nightmares"

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: Mr. Van Driessen sends the boys on a petition drive to stop a furrier from coming to town. videos: Hall & Oates - "Maneater" Def Leppard - "Animal" Huey Lewis & The News - "I Want A New Drug" The Dead Milkmen - "Smokin' Banana Peels" Young MC - "Principal's Office" Red Hot Chili Peppers - "Show Me Your Soul"

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: Beavis struggles to refrain from copying his butt.

programme.title: Mike Judge's Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: Beavis and Butt-Head perform their first ever good deed by putting DDT in the punch at the school dance.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: Beavis and Butt-head hope the apocalypse helps them score.

programme.title: Pee-wee's Playhouse
programme.desc: Schooltime has never been so fun when Pee-wee is the teacher! There are no tests and no homework! Then there are some thoughts on the future as Pee-wee and friends discuss what they want to be when they grow up.

programme.title: Pee-wee's Playhouse
programme.desc: The secret word is "begin." It's Spring - Cowboy Curtis stops by and he and Pee-wee plant a seed and watch it grow. The King of Cartoons brings his wife (The Queen of Cartoons) and their new baby (The Prince of Cartoons) over and introduces them to Pee-wee.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Han fires Caroline when she insults his diner, so Max tries to mend their relationship.

programme.title: The Sopranos
programme.desc: While Christopher is away, Tony gives Adriana a ride home that proves disastrous. Meanwhile, the Soprano crew plays a game of 'telephone' with the story that turns it into something far more sinister when an enraged Christopher gets wind of it.

programme.title: The Sopranos
programme.desc: With her son's future at stake, Carmela puts in some extra time with his college advisor, Robert Wegler. Meanwhile, Tony Blundetto realizes it's hard doing business with strangers, and A.J. concludes there's no place like home.

programme.title: The Good Place
programme.desc: Jason visits with some people from his past while Chidi gets help in resolving a problem.

programme.title: The Good Place
programme.desc: Eleanor makes a startling discovery that tests her resolve, Tahani looks to make amends and Janet does some bonding.

programme.title: The Good Place
programme.sub-title: The Worst Possible Use of Free Will
programme.desc: Eleanor recalls some forgotten events from her past.

programme.title: House of the Dragon
programme.desc: Amid whispers of bad omens, the Greens consider how to fill a void on Aegon's Council, Jacaerys sets out on a rogue mission to strike a deal, and Daemon enlists Lord Willem Blackwood to help persuade the Brackens to bend the knee.

programme.title: Game of Thrones
programme.desc: Tyrion arrives at King's Landing to take his father's place as Hand of the King. Stannis Baratheon plans to take the Iron Throne for his own. Robb tries to decide his next move in the war. The Night's Watch arrive at the house of Craster.

programme.title: Game of Thrones
programme.desc: Arya makes friends with Gendry. Tyrion tries to take control of the Small Council. Theon arrives at his home, Pyke, in order to persuade his father into helping Robb with the war. Jon tries to investigate Craster's secret.

programme.title: House
programme.desc: A famous celebrity photographer suffers from a stroke while being pregnant at her early forties. House and his team have to deal with a great dilemma, save the mother or her unborn child.

programme.title: House
programme.desc: House and Cuddy are flying back to the US from an international conference in Singapore. While en route a mysterious disease strikes one passenger and an epidemic unfolds, causing House to diagnose in midair since they have missed the halfway mark by passing the north pole.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: At Marge’s insistence, Bart has a “play date” with Ralph. Bart suddenly sees the upside of this friendship when he discovers Chief Wiggum’s “master key” and goes to explore Springfield’s abandoned prison.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Bart has his ear pierced and gets an ear-ring but Homer confiscates it.Later Homer and some colleagues join the Naval Reserve and sail on a submarine,where Homer accidentally fires the captain out of the torpedo hatch and,left in charge,allows the sub to drift inti enemy waters. It is attacked and springs a leak but Homer saves the day by sealing it with Bart's ear-ring before returning home for a dishonorable discharge.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Facing an IRS audit, Homer agrees to help the feds bust Mr. Burns for a missing trillion-dollar bill. When Burns makes an anti-government stand, Homer flees with him to Cuba.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: During a prank that goes wrong Bart destroys Groundskeeper Willie's shack. At the same time lazy Homer is getting a helper monkey called Mojo to do his chores and Lisa is the anchor on a children's T.V. news show. When Bart joins her as co-presenter and they start to compete for stories she takes her revenge by sending him to the scrapyard where Willie is sleeping rough.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Homer feuds with the Springfield garbage men and decides to run for Sanitation Commissioner. After winning the election with a sleazy campaign, Homer can’t keep the extravagant promises he made to the town.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: After meeting film star Rainier Wolfcastle at the gym ,Homer is volunteered by Bart to climb the treacherous local mountain the Murderhorn,as a publicity stunt for energy bars after Wolfcastle pulls out.Homer makes the climb despite warnings from Grampa and the exit of his sherpas and,at the top,makes a grisly discovery which helps his descent.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Bart’s latest stunt thwarts Lisa’s plan to attend a museum exhibit. When she tries to go alone and takes the wrong bus, Homer sets out to find her.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Marge and Homer discover that,by making love in public places, their sex life takes on an exciting new twist. They are almost caught in the nude at the novelty putting green, which leads to their having to make their escape in an air balloon which, unfortunately for them, having flown over a church with a glass roof with Homer hanging from the basket, comes to a stop in a crowded football stadium.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Homer and Bart decide to get rich by selling cooking lard. At the same time Lisa is perturbed when Alex Whitney, a sophisticated new class-mate arrives at the school and becomes instantly popular. Lisa is tasked with organizing a school dance but after the lard has found its way onto school premises both Alex and Lisa have fun by throwing it at each other.

programme.title: WWE Raw
programme.desc: Mr. McMahon reveals his master plan to end "Stone Cold" Steve Austin's reign as WWE Champion, however Ken Shamrock may take care of things a little early when he challenges the Rattlesnake for his title. Dustin Runnels' religious crusade hits a road block when Val Venis gives a preview of his latest video. Plus, Mankind, Triple H, and more.

programme.title: Schitt's Creek
programme.desc: Johnny gets thrown into the raw milk business. Moira tries to connect with the townspeople. David gives style advice.

programme.title: Schitt's Creek
programme.desc: Moira is worried that racy photos from her past will jeopardize the campaign; Johnny asks for financial help; Alexis gets a job.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: The women and the diner gang prepare for the premiere of the film about Caroline's life; Caroline and Max contemplate their future at the diner.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Max invites Caroline to be her roommate, but their new friendship is jeopardised when Max's boyfriend flirts with Caroline.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Thinking she is being helpful, Caroline facilitates Max's breakup with Robbie.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: A battle erupts over a shirt after Max introduces Caroline to shopping at thrift stores; a sarcastic street artist flirts with Max.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Caroline asks Max to help her break into her former townhouse to retrieve her much-needed night guard.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Caroline and Max throw a '90's-themed party at the diner to attract rich hipsters and raise money for their cupcake business; Caroline is horrified when her rich ex-boyfriend comes into the diner.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Caroline is tired of sleeping on the couch, and purchases a build-it yourself Murphy Bed; Max tries to work up the courage to give her cupcake business card to Peach.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Caroline persuades Max to take a cupcake-decorating class.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: The two take a job cleaning a hoarder's apartment for the extra money; Max learns that Johnny has a secret.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Johnny's girlfriend has a devious plan in mind when she hires Max and Caroline to bake cupcakes for an art show featuring his work.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Caroline and Max spend Caroline's first broke holiday season working at a department store's Santaland to earn extra money.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Max and Caroline try to find Chestnut a job in order to get him lodging for the winter. Meanwhile, Peach, the mother of the twins Max babysits, auditions for "Real Housewives of TriBeCa".

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: When Max's stove breaks, Caroline tries to return some expensive rings to Barney's department store, but discovers her surname is no longer an asset at her former home away from home.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Caroline discovers the secret ingredient to Max's cupcakes whilst in the midst of her new obsession couponing.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Max and Caroline's new neighbor draws their suspicion.

programme.title: Ted Lasso
programme.desc: With their season in a tailspin, Richmond try to right the ship against mighty Manchester City. Off the pitch, everyone faces their own setbacks.

programme.title: Ted Lasso
programme.desc: A friendly match takes the team to Amsterdam, where one night out unlocks truths for many.

programme.title: Beakman's World
programme.desc: Investigating snow; natural selection.

programme.title: Beakman's World
programme.desc: The differences between alligators and crocodiles; a look at robots.

programme.title: Beakman's World
programme.sub-title: Geysers and Hot Springs, Beakmania & Kidneys
programme.desc: Geysers; the function of kidneys.

programme.title: Beakman's World
programme.desc: A look at sleep; what makes sounds loud and soft.

programme.title: Beakman's World
programme.desc: Lobsters and crabs; Swiss scientist Daniel Bernoulli.

programme.title: Bill Nye: The Science Guy
programme.desc: A plant’s recipe for food has only three main ingredients: sunlight, carbon dioxide, and water. When plants make their food, they give off what animals need – oxygen. Without oxygen, animals like us wouldn’t be able to breathe. If plants weren’t here on Earth, we wouldn’t be here.

programme.title: Bill Nye: The Science Guy
programme.desc: We live on top of rocks – the Earth’s surface. There are three basic types of rocks — igneous, sedimentary, and metamorphic — and each type is made a different way. Igneous rocks are made from cooled lava. Sedimentary rocks are made from small pieces of other rocks stuck together. Metamorphic rocks are formed when other rocks are heated or pressed, or both. One type of rock can change into another type of rock as the Earth’s surface shifts and changes. It takes the right conditions and a lot of time.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: The Wattersons play Dodge or Dare (Dodj or Daar).

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: Mom lets the Wattersons have it in a supermarket.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: When Gumball and Darwin receive a scary email, everyone is a suspect... including themselves!

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: Darwin has promised to play video games with Gumball, but there is a Banana Joe-shaped problem...

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: When Nicole is away "anything goes" and Richard's lack of rules soon attracts unwelcome house guests.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: Gumball and Darwin try to understand Juke's urgent beatbox message.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: A new girl, Sarah, causes trouble for Gumball and Darwin when she tries to get the guys from her old school to fight them. Gumball and Darwin aren't having it.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: Gumball decides to take down the Internet after he accidentally creates an embarrasing viral web video.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: The Watterson kids come up with a fool-proof plan to save Nicole, but is it Gumball-proof?

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: The life of the "inanimate" objects of Elmore are shown in a series of short skits.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: A flashback reveals how Robin changed from a mild-mannered sidekick to form the Teen Titans and become their quick-tempered leader.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: The continuation of a flashback that reveals how Robin changed from a mild-mannered sidekick to form the Teen Titans and become their quick-tempered leader.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: When the male Titans are injured, Raven and Starfire must become bros to stop the Bropocalypse.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: The Titans make Robin give them a tour of Wayne Manor and discover how fun it is to be rich.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: Batman is more interested in watching TV than participating in his birthday party.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: The Titans find it odd when Starfire becomes friends with an ice cream scoop.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: Beat Boy neglects his responsibilities, leaving the other Titans to raise his baby chick.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: The Titans are excited to see the movie version of their favorite show, "Babies vs. Dogs" until the movie's exposition makes watching it super-boring. In order to save the film, the Titans go into the movie and break up the talk with exciting action.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: Inspired by the "Babies vs Dogs" movie, the Titans decide to make a big Hollywood film of their own.

programme.title: The Ren & Stimpy Show
programme.desc: Ren and Stimpy join the Army, where they begin basic training under a very demanding Drill Sargent. We then follow the unusual and bizarre adventures of "Powdered Toast Man" as he is called on for various rescue missions.

programme.title: The Ren & Stimpy Show
programme.desc: Ren learns the benefits of dental hygiene the hard way, as he refuses to listen to Stimpy's advice on brushing your teeth. Then Ren and Stimpy are tossed in the pound, where they discover the true meaning of "going to sleep".

programme.title: The Ren & Stimpy Show
programme.desc: Ren and Stimpy hit the road as door-to-door Rubber Nipple Salesmen, peddling their wares to a bizarre clientele, including a psychotic Fire Chief, a paranoid Mr. Horse, and a family actually in need of new nipples!

programme.title: The Ren & Stimpy Show
programme.desc: Ren gets driven out of his mind yet again as his anxiously awaited cousin Svën forms a stupidity bond with Stimpy through magic Nose Goblins, Kitty Litter, and the "Don't Whiz on the Electric Fence" board game.

programme.title: The Ren & Stimpy Show
programme.desc: Ren and Stimpy visit a haunted mansion where a depressive ghost fruitlessly tries to scare them, but the clueless duo barely notice his antic... not even the chainsaw!

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: The boys sit in on a speech therapy class and improve their vocabulary.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: Recall a time when life was simpler as Beavis, Butt-head and other Highland residents fill in for the Cleaver family.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: Butt-head rushes Beavis to the emergency room after an accident involving smoke, fire...and Beavis' butt.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: Mr. Buzzcut demands a report about our country's forefathers from the boys; they imagine themselves as various figures in American history.

programme.title: Double Dare
programme.sub-title: Comets (Johanna & Daniel) vs. Blue Hawks (Judy & Chris)

programme.title: You Can't Do That on Television
programme.desc: The shows sees its last of Mr. L. Dime and all the CJOH jokes.

programme.title: You Can't Do That on Television
You Can't Do That on Television
programme.desc:
The Muppets Get Suffed will not be seen at this time so that we may present...
Christine's dad makes her do work while hosting the show, so she keeps shovels a wheelbarrow full of dirt onto the set.

programme.title: You Can't Do That on Television
programme.desc:
The Dukes of Hazard Line Up for Gas will not be seen at this time so that we may present...
Ross spends most of the show making Christine's life miserable by making her do many strange things like jump from an airplane without a parachute, forcing her to be shot, makes her sick by distorting the broadcast, has her stuffed into a trash can, and even has her mummified.

programme.title: You Can't Do That on Television
programme.desc:
Captain Kangaroo Robs a Bank will not be seen at this time so that we may present...
Christine goes on strike demanding a raise. She eventually gets it, but discovers that she won't get paid for the time she was on strike, even though she was still on the show.

programme.title: You Can't Do That on Television
programme.desc:
Jaws meets the Love Boat will not be seen at this time so that we may present...
Christine spends the entire show waiting for her boyfriend to call her, which he doesn't.

programme.title: MXC
programme.desc: Hi-Tech vs. Civil Service. Hi-Tech computer nerds take on the Government Civil Servants. It's a bombastic brawl pitting brilliance vs. bureaucracy, MXC style. Games: Dope on a Rope, Circle Jerkers, Swamp Gassers, and Brass Balls.

programme.title: MXC
programme.sub-title: Cable TV Workers vs. White House Employees
programme.desc:
Finding out who's more ruthless: cable TV workers or White House employees. Meanwhile, Kenny remembers a White House tour.
Games include: Rotating Surfboards of Death; Door Jam; Mine Games; Tumbling Dominoes of Doom

programme.title: MXC
programme.desc:
Real life and wild life battle it out like animals on this episode of MXC, as reality TV stars face off against animal specialists.
Games include: Window Pain; Dash to Death; Loogie Launch; Endangering Species; Log Drop

programme.title: MXC
programme.desc:
The battle looms between office workers and toy makers in this MXC battle.
Games include: Holes of Glory; Skidmarkers; Log Drop; Chum in the Mouth

programme.title: MXC
programme.desc:
Can the military take down the big lacquered hair, perfect bodies and personalities of the beauty pageant contestants?
Games include: Sinkers and Floaters; Turtle Hurdlers Crossing the Poo-Tomac; Pole Riders; Boulder Dash

programme.title: MXC
programme.desc:
Hoteliers go head-to-head with entrepreneurs in this battle. Captain Tenneal speaks on the dangers of hotel rooms.
Games include: Dirty Muddy Balls; Saddle Sores; Staff Infectors; Rotating Surfboard of Death

programme.title: MXC
programme.desc:
In this episode, former Olympians from America battle former Olympians from the rest of the world. Kenny and Vic try to conceal their USA pride. The Captain hangs out with pop sensation Pey'once.
Games include: Sinkers and Floaters; Dope on a Rope; Dash to Death; Log Drop

programme.title: MXC
programme.desc:
Members of entertainment media take on union workers on this episode of MXC. Vic and Kenny discuss the benefits of union employment.
Games include: Window Pain; Irritable Bowl Syndrome; Muddy Runs; Tumbling Dominoes of Doom

programme.title: Daria
programme.desc: The members of Mystic Spiral are traumatized by an exploding fog machine.

programme.title: Daria
programme.desc: Another family dinner at the Morgendorffer household.

programme.title: Daria
programme.desc: Upchuck impresses Kevin and Brittany with a card trick.

programme.title: Daria
programme.desc: The Morgendorffer family prepares for some sibling rivalry between Helen and her sister Rita.

programme.title: Daria
programme.desc: Daria, Jodie and Upchuck are in the running for a top scholarship prize. Unfortunatley, this competition makes Jane feel left out(see "See Jane Run," Episode #2.11), and to make matters worse, she finds that the company that sponsors the scholarship has a record of discriminatory hiring practices. Will the Lawndale brainiac compromise her principles and learn how to be a kiss-up?

programme.title: Daria
programme.desc: Daria and Tom enjoy a not-so-quiet evening at home.

programme.title: Daria
programme.desc: Daria becomes obsessed with a big carton. Who wouldn't?

programme.title: Daria
programme.desc: Daria and Quinn arrive at their new school. Quinn makes friends easily. Our Daria does not. We meet Daria's teachers, her family, and her new best friend, Jane.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Top secret blueprints for a world shattering device have been stolen from Puttinghamdown Research station by Mac the Fork. He teams up with Dudley Poyson to build it as Dangermouse and Penfold race to Glasgow to stop them.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: London has been flooded. Indeed, all the sea and land on Earth have swapped places. It appears to be because the moon's gravitational pull has gone haywire, so DM and Penfold head up into outer space.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: London has been shrouded in darkness for almost a week, so Danger Mouse and Penfold head off into outer space to investigate. There they encounter a anti-logic spaceship manned by The Gremlin, who plans to turn the entire Earth Topsy turvy.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Stiletto steals a batch of recently developed passport pictures of Penfold. Il Barone proceeds to use his new puppet personality projector to take over Penfolds' mind, what little there is of it, and turn him into an assassin.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.sub-title: I Spy With My Little Eye...
programme.desc: Danger Mouse and Penfold are sent to the Arctic when Greenback threatens to melt the polar ice with sun lamps.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Danger Mouse and Penfold are sent to track down a huge hairy monster that has been crushing the local villages. When they find it they discover he's actually friendly, but suffers from extreme bouts of sore-feet. They get help from the RCMP - Royal Canadian Mounted Podiatrists.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Danger Mouse and Penfold get a visit from Penfold's aunt, but while they were having time with her, she is suddenly snatched away, along with the Statue of Liberty from the U.S.A. D.M. and Penfold soon realize that Greenback is behind the theft and the kidnapping, and so it's up to them to stop Greenback and save Penfold's aunt, being very careful of the dangers they soon face.

programme.title: Trading Places
programme.desc: A snobbish Wall Street commodities broker and a wily street hustler find their positions reversed as part of a bet by two callous millionaire brothers who run a brokerage house.

programme.title: My Cousin Vinny
programme.desc: Two carefree pals traveling through rural Alabama on their way back to college are mistakenly arrested and charged with murder. Fortunately, one of them has a cousin who's a lawyer - Vincent Gambini, a former auto mechanic from Brooklyn who has just passed his bar exam after his sixth try. When he arrives with his leather-clad girlfriend to try his first case, it's a real shock - for him and the Deep South!

programme.title: Click
programme.desc: A married workaholic, Michael Newman doesn't have time for his wife and children, not if he's to impress his ungrateful boss and earn a well-deserved promotion. So when he meets Morty, a loopy sales clerk, he gets the answer to his prayers: a magical remote that allows him to bypass life's little distractions with increasingly hysterical results.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head Do the Universe
programme.desc: After a "creative" judge sentences them to space camp, a black hole sends our adolescent heroes 24 years into the modern future where the duo misuse iPhones, embark on a quest to score, and become targets of the Deep State.

programme.title: Prince of Space
programme.desc: A Group of space men from the planet Krankor who resemble chickens are led by their leader, Phantom to invade Earth. But a strange superhero named Prince of Space (actually a bootblack in disguise) arrives to defeat the spacemen. Although the weapons of the aliens cannot harm the Prince of Space, the Phantom continues to fight, and many plot twists (including capturing a group of elderly scientists) ensue. Watch out for the Giant!

programme.title: Police Academy: Mission to Moscow
programme.desc: The Russians seek help in dealing with the Mafia from the veterans of the Police Academy. They head off to Moscow, in order to find evidence against Konstantin Konali, who marketed a computer game that everyone in the world is playing.

programme.title: Police Academy
programme.desc: The mayor declares that anyone can enroll in the police academy irrespective of their physical condition or education level, and many misfits descend on the academy, including a young troublemaker who's forced to apply or go to jail.

programme.title: Police Academy 2: Their First Assignment
programme.desc: Six Police Academy graduates are assigned to the 16th precinct, and their Captain desperately wants them to clean up the crime ridden neighborhood, but his second in command schemes to become captain himself by sabotaging the rookies.

programme.title: The Princess Bride
programme.desc: In this enchantingly cracked fairy tale, the beautiful Princess Buttercup and the dashing Westley must overcome staggering odds to find happiness amid six-fingered swordsmen, murderous princes, Sicilians and rodents of unusual size. But even death can't stop these true lovebirds from triumphing.

programme.title: Hot Shots!
programme.desc: Topper Harley, a top gun fighter pilot, is recalled to serve on the SS Essess. Topper's mission is to destroy Saddam Hussein's nuclear plants. Unfortunately, Topper is psychologically imbalanced and is sure to crack under pressure.

programme.title: Abbott and Costello Meet the Mummy
programme.desc: In Egypt Peter and Freddie find the archaeologist Dr. Zoomer murdered before they can return to America. A medallion leads them to a crypt where a revived mummy provides the terror.

programme.title: Ace Ventura: Pet Detective
programme.desc: To be a Pet Detective, you have to understand both the criminals and animals. Ace Ventura goes even further... He behaves like a criminal animal. When a football team's mascot (a dolphin) is stolen just before the Superbowl, Ace Ventura is put on the case. Now, who would want to steal a dolphin, and why?

programme.title: Grown Ups
programme.desc: After their high school basketball coach passes away, five good friends and former teammates reunite for a Fourth of July holiday weekend.

programme.title: Borat
programme.desc: Kazakh journalist Borat Sagdiyev travels to America to make a documentary. As he zigzags across the nation, Borat meets real people in real situations with hysterical consequences. His backwards behavior generates strong reactions around him exposing prejudices and hypocrisies in American culture.

programme.title: PCU
programme.desc: Nervous high school senior Tom Lawrence visits Port Chester University, where he gets a taste of politically correct college life when he's guided by fraternity wild man Droz and his housemates at The Pit. But Droz and his pals have rivals in nasty preppy Rand McPherson and the school's steely president. With their house threatened with expulsion, Droz and company decide to throw a raging party where the various factions will collide.

programme.title: Billy Madison
programme.desc: In order to inherit his fed-up father Brian's hotel empire, immature and lazy Billy Madison must repeat grades one through twelve all over again. The further Billy progresses, the harder his hated rival Eric Gordon tries to stop him.
